title: TTL enforcement for audit logs store (retention policy wiring + purge job)
preview: Implements retention/TTL enforcement for the audit logs storage layer and wires it into the control-plane retention policy evaluator. This is a core dependency for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” project (ENG-18434). Summary: (1) Adds a background purge job for audit logs using the purge job fra...
text:
Implements retention/TTL enforcement for the audit logs storage layer and wires it into the control-plane retention policy evaluator. This is a core dependency for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” project (ENG-18434). Summary: (1) Adds a background purge job for audit logs using the purge job framework (PR #28489). (2) Evaluates effective retention policy per tenant + region + data class (audit_logs) via the control plane evaluator (ADR-017 / PR #28427). (3) Enforces safe defaults and guardrails: minimum TTL by tier, optional legal hold override (no deletes while hold is active), and deletion safety limits to avoid DB pressure. (4) Adds verification metrics for TTL lag + deletion counts + failures with labels constrained to avoid cardinality blowups. (5) Adds integration tests covering policy changes, cache invalidation, and idempotent purge runs.

Implementation d...

title: Control plane: API endpoints for route-level quantization settings (CRUD + RBAC + audit log)
preview:
Motivation
We need a control-plane API surface to store and manage route-level quantization settings for the Quantization Profile Library (v1). This PR adds the first pass at CRUD endpoints, enforces RBAC, and emits audit records so changes are attributable and reversible.

Scope
- Adds RouteQuantizationSettings res...
text:
Motivation
We need a control-plane API surface to store and manage route-level quantization settings for the Quantization Profile Library (v1). This PR adds the first pass at CRUD endpoints, enforces RBAC, and emits audit records so changes are attributable and reversible.

Scope
- Adds RouteQuantizationSettings resource and persistence (backed by existing route_config storage).
- API endpoints:
 - GET /v1/routes/{route_id}/quantization-settings
 - PUT /v1/routes/{route_id}/quantization-settings (upsert)
 - DELETE /v1/routes/{route_id}/quantization-settings (reset to default)
 - GET /v1/orgs/{org_id}/routes/quantization-settings (bulk list for Console)
- RBAC: restricted to org_owner, org_admin, and route_admin roles (same authorization model used for route config edits).
- Audit logging: emits an audit record on create/update/delete with actor, org_id, route_id, and diff summary.
- V...

title: Introduce policy-orchestrator: temporal RBAC boundaries + KMS evidence hasher
preview: Motivation: Production RBAC v2 policies are increasingly dynamic (timebound grants, operator leases, cross-region operator exceptions). We need an orchestrator that can express temporal RBAC boundaries, translate policy decisions into KMS-scoped evidence, and emit compact, signed audit envelopes that can be consumed...
text: Motivation: Production RBAC v2 policies are increasingly dynamic (timebound grants, operator leases, cross-region operator exceptions). We need an orchestrator that can express temporal RBAC boundaries, translate policy decisions into KMS-scoped evidence, and emit compact, signed audit envelopes that can be consumed by downstream retention/compliance tooling. This PR introduces a new higher-level 'policy-orchestrator' service and several runtime integrations: 1) Temporal RBAC interpreter (supports TTL-based grants and revocation windows), 2) KMS evidence hasher (computes a deterministic hash of claim bundles and attaches KMS-wrapped attestations so audit stores can verify provenance without storing raw tokens), 3) Audit bridge sharding (splits signed audit envelopes by residency/geo policy and emits metadata for retention pipelines), and 4) Dry-run and canary rollouts for existing RBA...

title: Add benchmark serializer and release timeliner
preview: Why: onboarding produces many per-variant benchmark artifacts (latency histograms, P50/P95 traces, throughput stamps). We need a compact, versioned on-disk format so the registry can store, index, and compose those artifacts into human-facing release notes and machine-checkable regression timelines. This PR introduc...
text:
Why: onboarding produces many per-variant benchmark artifacts (latency histograms, P50/P95 traces, throughput stamps). We need a compact, versioned on-disk format so the registry can store, index, and compose those artifacts into human-facing release notes and machine-checkable regression timelines. This PR introduces a small, portable 'benchmark serializer' and a 'release timeliner' service that composes micro-release entries into a chronological digest for changelogs and downgrade/rollout playbooks.

What: single PR that implements: (1) bench serializer (binary+JSON compact representation with v1 header and stable field ordering); (2) timeliner CLI and library that consumes serialized benchmark artifacts and emits a microrelease timeline (JSONL) and a condensed markdown digest for release notes; (3) CI hook to assemble timeline artifacts and attach a release digest draft to the line...

title: Thrift Sherpa: guided batching and quantization calibrator
preview: Context: customers require lower per-token cost without sacrificing tail latency or regressions in output quality. Existing autotuners either over-aggressively quantize or recommend batch sizes that increase p95 for small requests. This PR introduces 'Thrift Sherpa', a guided calibrator that combines sampled product...
text: Context: customers require lower per-token cost without sacrificing tail latency or regressions in output quality. Existing autotuners either over-aggressively quantize or recommend batch sizes that increase p95 for small requests. This PR introduces 'Thrift Sherpa', a guided calibrator that combines sampled production traces, a lightweight simulator of RTT and KV-cache hit rates, and a conservative cost model to propose batching shapes and quant profiles per model + route. Design summary: 1) Offline trace ingestion: a small trace extractor that samples token lengths, sequence distributions, and route metadata. 2) Workload simulator: fast heuristics to estimate batching throughput, p50/p95, and token-level cost under candidate batch shapes and quant profiles. 3) Scoring and constraints: multi-objective score (cost reduction weighted vs. latency SLO and quality delta budget) with hard ...

title: router: add FPE decision tracing + enforce pinned-build fallback guardrails
introduce-adaptive-fidelity-curator-and-compact-failure-summarizer-ci-hook
preview: Motivation: Our existing CI smokechecks treat every prompt in a prompt-set equally which leads to noisy failures when a small subset of high-variance prompts trigger alerts. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: (1) Adaptive Fidelity Curator: a CI hook that assigns per-prompt fidelity weights using an exponenti...
text: Motivation: Our existing CI smokechecks treat every prompt in a prompt-set equally which leads to noisy failures when a small subset of high-variance prompts trigger alerts. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: (1) Adaptive Fidelity Curator: a CI hook that assigns per-prompt fidelity weights using an exponential-moving-average of recent evaluation scores and token-level stability signals, and exposes a soft-fail gating policy that scales alert severity by weighted SLO violations; (2) Compact Failure Summarizer: a post-failure analyzer that clusters failing examples by semantic/diff similarity, extracts exemplars, and produces a one-page failure summary with suggested roll-back scope (prompt IDs or prompt-cohort) for on-call triage. Changes in this PR are intended to reduce noisy alerts, prioritize high-impact regressions, and provide engineers with an immediate, digestible failu...

title: Introduce stochastic-consensus eval oracle and CI bridge for instability-aware gating
preview: Motivation: We see intermittent flakiness in prompt-set regressions that traditional deterministic smoke checks miss. This PR adds a stochastic consensus evaluator that runs multiple randomized perturbations/seed variations per prompt, computes a consensus distribution over binary/metric-level outcomes, and exposes ...
text: Motivation: We see intermittent flakiness in prompt-set regressions that traditional deterministic smoke checks miss. This PR adds a stochastic consensus evaluator that runs multiple randomized perturbations/seed variations per prompt, computes a consensus distribution over binary/metric-level outcomes, and exposes a probabilistic gate for CI. The goal is to reduce false positives (single-seed noise) while surfacing true instability and persistent regressions; it also provides quarantine suggestions for cohorts with high variance. What this implements: 1) eval_harness/oracles/stochastic_consensus.py - core evaluator implementing ensemble of perturbations, aggregation functions (consensus rate, trimmed mean, bootstrap CI), and an optional seed scheduler; 2) eval_harness/ci/consensus_ci_adapter.py - adapter that converts evaluator results into CI-friendly status with configurable thresh...

title: openai-invoke-affinity-annotations-and-fragment-serializer
preview: Motivation: Improve OpenAI-compat tool/function-call selection by exposing an 'invoke_affinity' annotation on tool metadata and by making streaming fragments self-descriptive so tool calls are never mis-routed or partially applied by downstream coalescers. Summary: 1) Add a non-breaking 'invoke_affinity' optional an...
text: Motivation: Improve OpenAI-compat tool/function-call selection by exposing an 'invoke_affinity' annotation on tool metadata and by making streaming fragments self-descriptive so tool calls are never mis-routed or partially applied by downstream coalescers. Summary: 1) Add a non-breaking 'invoke_affinity' optional annotation to the tool registry and mapper (values: high|normal|low|prefer_local) that is used by the OpenAI-compat selection heuristic to bias tool_choice without hard overrides. 2) Serialize tool invocation fragments emitted during streaming with a compact header (v1) containing id, affinity, and schema-hash so fragment coalescers and proxies can re-assemble or route fragments deterministically. 3) Update the compatibility layer to treat tool messages with affinity=high as preferred targets for low-latency lanes (dedicated pools) and affinity=prefer_local to prefer the call...

title: page-siloing-locality-preserving-evictor-and-spill-attenuation
preview: Context: Our KV cache has increasing fragmentation and frequent small spill writes under mixed workloads (short chat traces + occasional long contexts). Motivation: reduce tail latency and spill I/O amplification by keeping hot keys co-located in contiguous page 'silos' and by making the evictor prefer locality-pres...
text: Context: Our KV cache has increasing fragmentation and frequent small spill writes under mixed workloads (short chat traces + occasional long contexts). Motivation: reduce tail latency and spill I/O amplification by keeping hot keys co-located in contiguous page 'silos' and by making the evictor prefer locality-preserving demotions. This PR implements a page siloing allocator, a locality-preserving eviction policy, and a spill attenuation controller that defers and coalesces spill writes when under transient pressure. Changes are gated behind a runtime flag (kv.page_siloing_enabled) and incremental by default. Design summary: 1) Page siloing allocator groups allocations into silos by tenant and affinity score computed from recent access fingerprinting. Silos aim to keep related KV entries on contiguous page ranges to reduce fragmentation and sequentialize spill write patterns. 2) Loca...

title: Add multi-turn coherence smokehook and turnwise replay adapter
preview: Motivation: A growing class of regressions only surface in multi-turn scenarios (turn-to-turn contradiction, context leakage, or degeneration across turns). Existing single-turn smokechecks miss these. This PR adds a multi-turn coherence smokehook and a turnwise replay adapter to detect and gate regressions that man...
text:
Motivation: A growing class of regressions only surface in multi-turn scenarios (turn-to-turn contradiction, context leakage, or degeneration across turns). Existing single-turn smokechecks miss these. This PR adds a multi-turn coherence smokehook and a turnwise replay adapter to detect and gate regressions that manifest across conversational turns.

What this PR introduces: 1) A coherence metric that combines (a) cross-turn semantic consistency (embedding-based similarity between prior assistant turns and subsequent responses) and (b) next-turn prediction stability (model likelihoods / token-level divergence when conditioned on different plausible prior paraphrases). 2) A smokehook that runs a compact multi-turn golden set on every canary and nightly CI run; the hook returns softfail/quarantine when drift exceeds thresholds. 3) A turnwise replay adapter which can: (i) expand failing ...

title: Steady-state slew-rate prober and latency-heatmap serializer
preview: Context: Production traffic exhibits sudden arrival-rate changes that current benchmarks treat as either isolated spikes or steady-state; we lacked a lightweight prober to characterize slew (rate-of-change) and to mark steady-state windows for downstream aggregations. This PR introduces a 'slew-rate prober' that emi...
text: Context: Production traffic exhibits sudden arrival-rate changes that current benchmarks treat as either isolated spikes or steady-state; we lacked a lightweight prober to characterize slew (rate-of-change) and to mark steady-state windows for downstream aggregations. This PR introduces a 'slew-rate prober' that emits time-series markers and a compact latency-heatmap serializer to efficiently export per-window histograms for postprocessing. Motivation: Provide an automated way to (1) detect sustained ramp-up/down phases, (2) isolate true steady-state windows for p95/p99 comparison across builds, and (3) export telemetry in a bandwidth-efficient format so long-run batch analyses are cheaper. High-level changes: added a new python prober harness (slew_rate_prober.py) with a configurable EWMA-based slew estimator and a hysteresis-driven steady-state detector; implemented adaptive samplin...

title: sequence churn stressor and multiwindow latency mapper
preview: Context: We need a compact runner and reporting pipeline that exercises 'sequence churn'—the interaction between variable context window sizes, primer reuse, and short-lived KV cache entries that manifest as multi-window latency regressions in production. Motivation: Recent production traces showed p95 regressions c...
text: Context: We need a compact runner and reporting pipeline that exercises 'sequence churn'—the interaction between variable context window sizes, primer reuse, and short-lived KV cache entries that manifest as multi-window latency regressions in production. Motivation: Recent production traces showed p95 regressions correlated with bursts of short-context requests that evict hot KV entries, causing downstream longer-context requests to suffer. This PR introduces a workload generator (seq-churn-stressor) and a reporting component (multiwindow-latency-mapper) that: 1) reproduces realistic churn patterns across tenants and primer-length distributions, 2) collects per-window latency heatmaps (window sizes: 32, 64, 128, 256 tokens), 3) emits cohorted quantiles and an artifact CSV for cross-run comparison. Checklist: - [x] Implement stochastic primer/seed generator with tenant skew and TTL-dr...

title: Introduce mergestate-prober and slim release adapter to reduce merge-time regressions
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a small, cross-platform Go utility (tools/mergestate-prober) that evaluates merge readiness by inspecting recent CI flakiness, re-run counts, and commit graph churn; it also replaces the brittle shell-based release script with a slim, declarative release adapter (cmd/releasectl + releases...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a small, cross-platform Go utility (tools/mergestate-prober) that evaluates merge readiness by inspecting recent CI flakiness, re-run counts, and commit graph churn; it also replaces the brittle shell-based release script with a slim, declarative release adapter (cmd/releasectl + releases/*.yaml). Motivation: We've observed multiple late-stage regressions and noisy merges where intermittent CI flakiness or concurrent rollups caused post-merge rollbacks. The goal is not to gate merges aggressively, but to give maintainers a deterministic signal (and optional auto-quarantine) when a PR lands during high churn or when CI instability is present. What changed: 1) New tool: tools/mergestate-prober - Go binary that aggregates: recent check-run reruns, failure rates for top 5 flaky tests, and a small heuristic for merge collisions (multiple dependent PRs touching t...

title: delta-driven pin throttling and tiered reuse allocator for paged KV
preview: Motivation: Our paged KV block manager saw high tail latency and frequent pin-induced stalls when conversational workloads produce short bursts of focused hot prefixes. Existing heuristics pinned aggressively on first-detect hotness and coalesced eagerly, which increased contention and caused kernel scheduling jitte...
text: Motivation: Our paged KV block manager saw high tail latency and frequent pin-induced stalls when conversational workloads produce short bursts of focused hot prefixes. Existing heuristics pinned aggressively on first-detect hotness and coalesced eagerly, which increased contention and caused kernel scheduling jitter. This change introduces a delta-driven pin heuristic plus a lightweight tiered reuse allocator to smooth pin activity and defer expensive coalesces into the background.

title: add-perspective-weighted-eval-gate-and-selective-replay-hook
preview: Motivation: standard aggregate eval gates (mean / median-based) can mask systematic, perspective-driven regressions where a model drifts at the cohort-level for a particular annotator perspective (e.g. cultural framing, domain expertise). This PR introduces a lightweight perspective-weighted scoring function and a C...
text:
Motivation: standard aggregate eval gates (mean / median-based) can mask systematic, perspective-driven regressions where a model drifts at the cohort-level for a particular annotator perspective (e.g. cultural framing, domain expertise). This PR introduces a lightweight perspective-weighted scoring function and a CI gate that uses stratified bootstrapped confidence intervals across perspective clusters to surface regressions that would otherwise be attenuated by averaging. It also adds a selective-replay hook that schedules targeted re-evals for failing perspectives with deterministic sampling for reproducibility.

What this change contains: perspective clustering (unsupervised seed + optional annotation tag), per-perspective metric aggregation, perspective-weighted composite score, CI gate implementation wired into the existing CI hooks, and a selective replay orchestration that bac...

title: OpenAI /v1/chat.completions parity: stop-weighting, logprob fallbacks, and structured response shaping
preview: Context: Customers expect the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions semantics (stop arrays, role-preserving tokenization heuristics, and logprob fallbacks for truncated streams) to behave the same when using Redwood's compatibility layer. We observed two recurring issues in production: 1) ambiguous stop precedence when multip...
text: Context: Customers expect the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions semantics (stop arrays, role-preserving tokenization heuristics, and logprob fallbacks for truncated streams) to behave the same when using Redwood's compatibility layer. We observed two recurring issues in production: 1) ambiguous stop precedence when multiple stop tokens and stop weights are provided (edge cases where streaming fragments cause stray punctuation to trigger a premature stop), and 2) structured output/toolcall shapes drifting when streaming backpressure causes partial JSON fragments, leading downstream parsers to misinterpret tool metadata. This PR unifies parameter handling and adds a robust, streaming-aware logprob fallback path plus a structured-output flattener to keep toolcall metadata stable across chunk boundaries. Summary of changes: - Add `stop_weights` merging logic in the v1 param adapter: when caller...

title: introduce-route-auth-hint-header-and-compat-adapter-for-proxied-streaming-tool-calls
preview: Motivation: streaming tool calls and chained function/tool workflows suffer from fragile header semantics when requests are forwarded through edge proxies and gateway adapters (CORS preflight and streaming handshake especially). This PR adds a lightweight, opt-in per-route hint header (X-RW-Route-Auth) and a small c...
text: Motivation: streaming tool calls and chained function/tool workflows suffer from fragile header semantics when requests are forwarded through edge proxies and gateway adapters (CORS preflight and streaming handshake especially). This PR adds a lightweight, opt-in per-route hint header (X-RW-Route-Auth) and a small compatibility adapter for OpenAI-compat endpoints so downstream proxies can safely forward scoped auth tokens and a short-lived signing bridge header. Design: 1) X-RW-Route-Auth (value: hint tokens or route id) allows the API gateway to advertise per-route auth expectations to downstream workers without exposing project keys. 2) RW-Signed-Bridge is an asymmetric-signed envelope (opaque string) the gateway can attach when a request needs to be proxied to an external tool runtime; workers validate the signature against a rotation-aware public key store. 3) Backwards compatible...

title: stabilize-streaming-handshake-and-tool-handoff-with-role-serializer
preview: Motivation: production incidents and internal fuzzing uncovered multiple edge cases in the /v1/chat.completions compatibility layer during streaming: (1) mixed stop tokens and framed JSON tool outputs would cause stream boundary misalignment, (2) role aliasing (user/assistant/function/tool) metadata was being lost d...
text: Motivation: production incidents and internal fuzzing uncovered multiple edge cases in the /v1/chat.completions compatibility layer during streaming: (1) mixed stop tokens and framed JSON tool outputs would cause stream boundary misalignment, (2) role aliasing (user/assistant/function/tool) metadata was being lost during the stream->finalize transition leading to incorrect attribution in logs/metrics, and (3) logprob fallbacks were omitted when structured responses were negotiated in framed-json mode. This PR introduces a lightweight role serializer and a deterministic stop normalization pipeline to achieve parity with OpenAI /v1 semantics while preserving Redwood streaming guarantees. Summary of changes and rationale: 1) role serializer (api/compat/role_serializer.py): canonicalizes incoming message roles and preserves arbitrary role metadata in a compact JSON envelope attached to st...

title: tool-annotations-stable-choice-serialization-and-fragment-safe-streaming
preview: Context: Customers reported rare nondeterministic tool selection during streamed OpenAI-compatible responses where the model returned multiple candidate tool choices split across streaming fragments. This caused downstream systems to sometimes pick different tools after reassembly. Motivation: make the OpenAI adapte...
text: Context: Customers reported rare nondeterministic tool selection during streamed OpenAI-compatible responses where the model returned multiple candidate tool choices split across streaming fragments. This caused downstream systems to sometimes pick different tools after reassembly. Motivation: make the OpenAI adapter produce deterministic, replay-safe tool_choice selection and deliver tool payloads with fragment-safe markers so reassembly cannot change choice. What this PR does: 1) Introduces a canonical serialization path for tool choice and tool descriptors: ToolDescriptor v2 with canonical field ordering and stable hashing. This prevents dict-order nondeterminism when computing tool signatures used by routable policies. 2) Adds lightweight per-chunk framing markers in the streaming envelope to ensure the boundary between a tool_choice event and its associated tool payload cannot be...
